practicals.title: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing out glass tube; Boring a cork)
practicals.details: Micro-chemical methods should be used wherever possible.

practicals.title: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances (Melting/Boiling point, Crystallization)
practicals.details: Determination of melting/boiling points; Crystallization of Alum, Copper Sulphate, or Benzoic Acid.

practicals.title: Experiments based on pH
practicals.details: Determination of pH using universal indicator/pH paper; Comparing strong/weak acids; Study of common-ion effect.

practicals.title: Chemical Equilibrium studies (Shift in equilibrium)
practicals.details: Study shift between ferric ions/thiocyanate or [Co(H2O)6]2+/chloride ions by changing concentrations.

practicals.title: Quantitative Estimation (Titration)
practicals.details: Preparation of standard solutions; Determination of strength of NaOH/HCl using Oxalic acid/Sodium Carbonate.

practicals.title: Qualitative Analysis
practicals.details: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt; Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds.

practicals.title: Surface Chemistry (Sol preparation)
practicals.details: Preparation of lyophilic and lyophobic sols; Dialysis; Study of emulsifying agents.

practicals.title: Chemical Kinetics
practicals.details: Effect of concentration/temperature on reaction rate (Sodium Thiosulphate/HCl); Clock reactions.

practicals.title: Thermochemistry
practicals.details: Enthalpy of dissolution, neutralization, or interaction between Acetone and Chloroform.

practicals.title: Electrochemistry (Cell potential variation)
practicals.details: Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with change in electrolyte concentration.

practicals.title: Chromatography
practicals.details: Separation of pigments from leaves/flowers; Separation of constituents in an inorganic mixture.

practicals.title: Preparation of Inorganic Compounds
practicals.details: Preparation of double salts (Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate, Potash Alum) or Potassium Ferric Oxalate.

practicals.title: Preparation of Organic Compounds
practicals.details: Preparation of Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow, or 2-Naphthol Aniline dye.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups
practicals.details: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups.

practicals.title: Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins
practicals.details: Detection in pure samples and given foodstuffs.

projects.title: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

domain_1_remembering_and_understanding.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.

domain_2_applying.description: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

domain_3_analysing_evaluating_and_creati.description: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

general_rules: No chapter wise weightage is provided; care to be taken to cover all chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all sections.

class_xi_projects_suggested: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water
Study of methods of purification of water
Testing hardness and presence of ions in drinking water
Foaming capacity of washing soaps
Acidity of tea leaves
Rate of evaporation of liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers
Acidity of fruit/vegetable juices

Quantity of casein in milk samples
Preparation and comparison of soybean milk with natural milk
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase (pH/temperature effect)
Comparative study of fermentation rates
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi
Study of common food adulterants

domain_2_applying.description: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

domain_3_analysing_evaluating_and_creati.description: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_groups.title: Fundamental Concepts and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Ability to understand the nature of matter, apply laws of chemical combination, perform stoichiometric calculations, and handle measurement uncertainties.
competencies: Explain the importance of Chemistry in various disciplines and career paths.
Distinguish between macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic representations of chemical phenomena.
Apply Dalton's Atomic Theory to explain atomic and molecular masses.
Perform stoichiometric calculations involving mole concept, molar mass, percentage composition, and limiting reagents.
Identify sources of uncertainty in measurement and apply rules for significant figures.

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Understanding the evolution from classical models to quantum mechanical descriptions, including electronic configurations.
competencies: Trace the historical development of atomic models leading to the Bohr model.
Explain the postulates of the Bohr model for hydrogen atoms and its limitations.
Describe the transition towards the Quantum Mechanical Model of the atom.
Apply quantum numbers to describe electronic configurations.

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify elements based on electronic configuration, understand the modern periodic law, and predict trends in properties.
competencies: Justify the need for classification of elements and explain the genesis of periodic tables.
Apply the Modern Periodic Law to determine element positions and nomenclature (Z > 100).
Correlate electronic configuration with s, p, d, f block classifications.
Analyze periodic trends in atomic/ionic radii, ionization enthalpy, electron gain enthalpy, electronegativity, and valency.
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Mastery of various bonding theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) to predict molecular geometry and stability.
competencies: Differentiate between ionic, covalent, and coordinate bonds using Kossel-Lewis approach.
Predict molecular shapes using VSEPR theory based on electron pair repulsion.
Explain hybridization concepts (sp, sp2, sp3) and their relation to bond parameters.
Apply Valence Bond Theory and Molecular Orbital Theory to explain bonding in homonuclear diatomic molecules.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamic Principles and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze energy changes in chemical processes, calculate enthalpy variations, and determine reaction spontaneity.
competencies: Define thermodynamic terms (system, surroundings, state functions) and classify systems.
Calculate internal energy change (∆U) and enthalpy change (∆H) using calorimetry data.
Apply Hess's Law to determine reaction enthalpies for various types of reactions.
Relate Gibbs Energy Change (∆G) to spontaneity and equilibrium conditions.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Equilibrium Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Understanding dynamic equilibria in physical and chemical processes, including ionic equilibrium and buffer systems.
competencies: Distinguish between homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria and apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium.
Calculate equilibrium constants (K) and relate them to reaction quotient (Q) and Gibbs Energy (G).
Predict factors affecting equilibrium shifts according to Le Chatelier's principle.
Analyze ionic equilibrium in solutions, including acid-base ionization, buffer action, and solubility product.

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Ability to identify oxidation-reduction processes, balance redox equations, and understand electrode reactions.
competencies: Define oxidation and reduction in terms of electron transfer and classical ideas.
Assign oxidation numbers using established rules to identify oxidizing and reducing agents.
Balance complex redox reactions using the ion-electron method or oxidation number method.
Explain the role of redox reactions in electrode processes.

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Competence in nomenclature, isomerism, reaction mechanisms, purification techniques, and quantitative analysis of organic compounds.
competencies: Apply IUPAC rules for naming organic compounds and identifying structural representations.
Differentiate between various types of isomers (structural, stereoisomers).
Analyze fundamental concepts in organic reaction mechanisms including electron movement and intermediates.
Select appropriate methods for purification and qualitative/quantitative analysis of organic substances.
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Understanding the structure, properties, and reactions of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic hydrocarbons.
competencies: Classify hydrocarbons based on saturation and structural features (aliphatic vs aromatic).
Predict physical properties and chemical reactivity patterns for saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons.
Explain the structure of benzene and its unique stability compared to other cyclic compounds.
Assess environmental impacts such as carcinogenicity and toxicity associated with specific hydrocarbon classes.

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Ability to express solution concentrations, understand vapor pressure phenomena, and apply colligative properties for molar mass determination.
competencies: Calculate various expressions of concentration (molarity, molality, mole fraction) in solutions.
Explain Raoult's Law and deviations from ideal behavior in liquid-liquid solutions.
Apply colligative property laws to determine molar masses of solutes.
Distinguish between normal and abnormal molecular masses based on association/dissociation.

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry Applications
competency_groups.description: Understanding electrochemical cells, Nernst equation applications, conductance phenomena, and corrosion mechanisms.
competencies: Differentiate between galvanic and electrolytic cells and explain their working principles.
Apply the Nernst Equation to calculate cell potential under non-standard conditions.
Analyze factors affecting conductivity in electrolytic solutions (molar conductance, specific conductance).
Explain practical applications including batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion prevention.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics Analysis
competency_groups.description: Ability to determine reaction rates, derive integrated rate laws, and analyze factors influencing reaction speed.
competencies: Define and calculate the average and instantaneous rates of chemical reactions.
Derive and apply zero-order and first-order integrated rate equations.
Analyze temperature dependence of reaction rates using Arrhenius equation concepts.
Explain collision theory and activation energy requirements for effective collisions.

competency_groups.title: d-Block and f-Block Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Understanding the general properties, electronic configurations, and chemical behavior of transition elements and inner transition metals.
competencies: Explain the position of d-block and f-block elements in the periodic table based on configuration.
Analyze trends in physical and chemical properties (oxidation states, magnetic properties) across periods/groups.
Describe important compounds and applications of Transition Elements, Lanthanoids, and Actinoids.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry Principles
competency_groups.description: Mastery of Werner's theory, nomenclature, isomerism, bonding theories (Valence Bond & MO), and applications of coordination compounds.
competencies: Apply IUPAC rules for naming complex coordination compounds.
Identify different types of isomerism in coordination complexes (structural and stereoisomers).
Explain bonding characteristics using Valence Bond Theory, Crystal Field Theory concepts, and Metal Carbonyls.

competency_groups.title: Organic Functional Group Chemistry (Haloalkanes/Ethers)
competency_groups.description: Understanding nomenclature, preparation methods, physical properties, and substitution/elimination reactions of halo compounds and ethers.
competencies: Classify haloalkanes and haloarenes based on structure and hybridization.

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols, and Ethers Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Ability to distinguish between alcohols, phenols, and ethers based on structure and predict their chemical reactions.
competencies: Differentiate the acidic nature of phenol compared to alcohol.

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Compounds Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Understanding the chemistry of aldehydes and ketones including preparation methods, physical properties, and nucleophilic addition reactions.
competencies: Distinguish between aldehydes and ketones based on functional group structure.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Unit 8: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids

competency_groups.title: Amines Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Knowledge of amine classification, nomenclature, basicity trends, diazonium salts chemistry, and their synthetic utility.
competencies: Classify amines as primary, secondary, or tertiary based on structure.

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules and Biochemistry Basics
competency_groups.description: Understanding the structure, classification, and biological functions of carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, and hormones.
competencies: Classify biomolecules into monosaccharides, disaccharides, polysaccharides.

competency_groups.title: Practical Laboratory Skills and Safety
competency_groups.description: Competence in performing standard laboratory techniques, handling apparatus safely, conducting volumetric analysis, salt analysis, and executing investigatory projects.
competencies: Demonstrate proficiency in basic glassware manipulation (cutting, bending, boring).
Perform quantitative estimations using titration methods with high accuracy.

domain_1_remembering_and_understanding.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.

domain_2_applying.description: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

domain_3_analysing_evaluating_and_creati.description: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

general_rules: No chapter wise weightage is provided; care to be taken to cover all chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all sections.

weightages_by_unit.name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

level_3_analysing_evaluating_and_creatin.description: Examine information, make judgments, and propose solutions.

internal_assessment.policy: No overall choice in the question paper.
internal_assessment.choice_mechanism: 33% internal choices will be given in all sections.

formative_content_class_xii.topic: Surface Chemistry - Adsorption and Colloidal State
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

key_experiments: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances (Melting/Boiling point, Crystallization)
Tests for functional groups and foodstuffs

class_xi_projects_suggested: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water
Study of methods of purification of water
Testing hardness and presence of ions in drinking water
Foaming capacity of washing soaps
Acidity of tea leaves
Rate of evaporation of liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers
Acidity of fruit/vegetable juices
class_xii_projects_suggested: Presence of oxalate ions in guava at different ripening stages
Quantity of casein in milk samples
Preparation and comparison of soybean milk with natural milk
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase (pH/temperature effect)
Comparative study of fermentation rates
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi
Study of common food adulterants

projects.title: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of the methods of purification of water
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

practicals.title: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing out glass tube; Boring a cork)
practicals.details: Micro-chemical methods should be used wherever possible.

practicals.title: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances (Melting/Boiling point, Crystallization)
practicals.details: Determination of melting/boiling points; Crystallization of Alum, Copper Sulphate, or Benzoic Acid.

practicals.title: Experiments based on pH
practicals.details: Determination of pH using universal indicator/pH paper; Comparing strong/weak acids; Study of common-ion effect.

practicals.title: Chemical Equilibrium studies (Shift in equilibrium)
practicals.details: Study shift between ferric ions/thiocyanate or [Co(H2O)6]2+/chloride ions by changing concentrations.

practicals.title: Quantitative Estimation (Titration)
practicals.details: Preparation of standard solutions; Determination of strength of NaOH/HCl using Oxalic acid/Sodium Carbonate.

practicals.title: Qualitative Analysis
practicals.details: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt; Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds.

practicals.title: Surface Chemistry (Sol preparation)
practicals.details: Preparation of lyophilic and lyophobic sols; Dialysis; Study of emulsifying agents.

practicals.title: Chemical Kinetics
practicals.details: Effect of concentration/temperature on reaction rate (Sodium Thiosulphate/HCl); Clock reactions.

practicals.title: Thermochemistry
practicals.details: Enthalpy of dissolution, neutralization, or interaction between Acetone and Chloroform.

practicals.title: Electrochemistry (Cell potential variation)
practicals.details: Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with change in electrolyte concentration.

practicals.title: Chromatography
practicals.details: Separation of pigments from leaves/flowers; Separation of constituents in an inorganic mixture.

practicals.title: Preparation of Inorganic Compounds
practicals.details: Preparation of double salts (Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate, Potash Alum) or Potassium Ferric Oxalate.

practicals.title: Preparation of Organic Compounds
practicals.details: Preparation of Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow, or 2-Naphthol Aniline dye.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups
practicals.details: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups.

practicals.title: Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins
practicals.details: Detection in pure samples and given foodstuffs.
